column1: 4 endpoint(s). Mock definitions — assume realistic data shapes.
Primary performance endpoint. Returns clicks, impressions, CTR, position grouped by dimensions. Single source of truth for organic search performance.

column5: Why it matters

column4: Property identifier — 'sc-domain:acme.com' for domain property or 'https://acme.com/' for URL-prefix property
column5: Which site are we querying?
When does the analysis window start?

column4: YYYY-MM-DD. Most recent data has 24-48 hour lag
column5: When does the analysis window end? How recent is the data?

column4: Subset of: query, page, country, device, searchAppearance, date
column5: What perspective do I want — by keyword, by URL, by country, by device, by SERP feature, or trend over time?

column4: web | image | video | news | discover | googleNews. Default 'web'
column5: Which search vertical — main web search vs Discover vs News vs Images?

column4: 'final' (default, fully processed) | 'all' (includes fresh 1-2 day data, may revise)
column5: Do I need today's preliminary numbers or do I want stable historical data?

column4: auto | byPage | byProperty. Determines how clicks are deduplicated
column5: Should I count clicks per landing page (multiple per query OK) or per property (one credit per impression)?
How many rows do I want back this call?
Where in the result set should I resume? (for paginating beyond 25K rows)

column4: Filter clauses with operators: equals, contains, notContains, notEquals, includingRegex, excludingRegex
column5: How do I narrow results — only branded queries, only mobile, only one country, only specific URL pattern?
Why it matters

column4: Dimension values in the order specified in input dimensions
column5: What is this row about — which keyword/page/country/device combo?

column4: Number of clicks from Google search to your site for this dimension combination
column5: How many people actually arrived from search? — direct traffic count

column4: Number of times your URL appeared in search results
column5: How visible was I? — exposure / how many SERPs included me
Of people who saw me, how many clicked? — title/meta effectiveness, snippet appeal

column4: Average ranking position weighted by impressions. 1-based, lower is better
column5: Where am I ranking on average? — primary ranking metric

column4: byPage | byProperty — how Google actually aggregated
column5: Did Google honor my requested aggregation, or did it fall back?

column1: Per-URL diagnostic. Returns indexation status, mobile usability, rich results detection, last crawl, canonical resolution. GSC URL Inspection equivalent.

column5: Why it matters

column4: Full URL to inspect, must belong to verified property
column5: Which specific page do I want to diagnose?
Which property does this URL belong to?
Which locale's index version to inspect?
Why it matters
At a glance — is this page healthy, broken, or somewhere in between?

column4: Free-text state e.g. 'Submitted and indexed', 'Crawled - currently not indexed', 'Discovered - currently not indexed'
column5: Why specifically is the page in this state? — diagnostic root cause
Is robots.txt blocking Googlebot from crawling this URL?
Is the page allowed to be indexed? If blocked, what is the blocker — meta tag, HTTP header, or robots?

column4: ISO 8601 timestamp of most recent Googlebot crawl
column5: How recently has Google looked at this page? Is the crawl stale?

column4: SUCCESSFUL | SOFT_404 | BLOCKED_ROBOTS_TXT | NOT_FOUND | ACCESS_DENIED | SERVER_ERROR | REDIRECT_ERROR | ACCESS_FORBIDDEN | BLOCKED_4XX | INTERNAL_CRAWL_ERROR | INVALID_URL
column5: When Google last tried to fetch this page, what happened? Is the server returning errors?

column4: URL Google chose as the canonical (master) version
column5: Which version of this page is Google treating as authoritative?

column4: URL declared by your rel=canonical tag
column5: Which version did I tell Google was canonical?
Did I declare this URL in any sitemap? Which one?

column4: Pages on the web Google found pointing to this URL
column5: Where did Google discover this URL from? — internal/external link discovery audit
Is this page usable on mobile?

column4: Each issue has rule (e.g. 'CONTENT_WIDER_THAN_SCREEN', 'CLICKABLE_ELEMENTS_TOO_CLOSE') and severity
column5: What specifically makes this page mobile-unfriendly?
Are my structured data declarations valid?

column4: Each item: richResultType (Article, FAQ, HowTo, Product, etc.), items array, issues array
column5: Which schema types did Google parse and are any of them broken?
If using AMP, is the AMP version valid?

column4: Direct URL to GSC web UI for this inspection
column5: Where do I go to dig deeper in the UI?

column1: List submitted sitemaps or fetch single sitemap status. Used for migration audits and sitemap health checks.

column5: Why it matters
Which property's sitemaps?
Which sitemap do I want detail on?

column4: URL of parent sitemap index — restricts list to children of this index
column5: Show me only the sitemaps inside this index file
Why it matters
Where does this sitemap live?

column4: Last time the sitemap was submitted via API or GSC UI
column5: When did I last tell Google about this sitemap?

column4: True if Google hasn't processed yet
column5: Is Google still crawling/processing this sitemap?

column4: True if this is a sitemap-of-sitemaps
column5: Does this contain other sitemaps inside it (vs being a leaf sitemap)?

column4: Last time Googlebot fetched the sitemap file
column5: When did Google last actually read this sitemap?
Is the sitemap healthy? Anything to fix?
Is the sitemap broken in any way?
What kind of URLs does this sitemap declare?
How many URLs did I tell Google about?

column4: Of those, how many Google has indexed
column5: What's my submission-to-indexation ratio? Where is Google ignoring my declarations?

column1: On-demand mobile-friendly test. Returns rendering issues and optional screenshot. Note: largely superseded by URL Inspection's mobileUsabilityResult.

column5: Why it matters

column4: Page URL to test (does not need to be in a verified property)
column5: Which page should we test for mobile usability?

column4: Include rendered mobile screenshot in response
column5: Do I want a visual of how the page looks rendered?
Why it matters
Bottom line — is this page mobile-friendly?

column4: USES_INCOMPATIBLE_PLUGINS | CONFIGURE_VIEWPORT | FIXED_WIDTH_VIEWPORT | SIZE_CONTENT_TO_VIEWPORT | USE_LEGIBLE_FONT_SIZES | TAP_TARGETS_TOO_CLOSE
column5: Which specific mobile-friendly rule did the page violate?

column4: URLs Googlebot couldn't fetch (blocked CSS, JS, images)
column5: Which resources are being blocked from Googlebot, breaking the rendered view?
What format is the screenshot?
What does the page actually look like rendered? — visual debugging
Did the test even run? — needed before trusting other fields
If the test failed, the specific reason (e.g., 'page not reachable', 'content wider than screen'). Useful for surfacing the fix in a report.
